input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI Shell app has these problems:
1. GoToAsync("productdetails") throws an exception
2. GoToAsync("//products/active") navigates but nothing visible changes
3. My app takes 3 seconds to start and I have 12 pages in Shell
What's likely wrong in each case and how do I fix them?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI Shell app has these problems:
1. GoToAsync("productdetails") throws an exception
2. GoToAsync("//products/active") navigates but nothing visible changes
3. My app takes 3 seconds to start and I have 12 pages in Shell
What's likely wrong in each case and how do I fix them?

data.content:
Here are the likely causes and fixes:

**1. `GoToAsync("productdetails")` throws an exception**

The route isn't registered. Non-visual (detail) routes must be explicitly registered:

```csharp
// In AppShell constructor or MauiProgram.cs
Routing.RegisterRoute("productdetails", typeof(ProductDetailsPage));
```

Routes defined in XAML (`<ShellContent Route="productdetails">`) are only reachable by their full URI. Ad-hoc navigation requires `RegisterRoute`.

---

**2. `GoToAsync("//products/active")` navigates but nothing renders**

The absolute route (`//`) matches the Shell structure, so no exception is thrown â€” but if the `ShellContent` for `active` has its `ContentTemplate` pointing to a page whose **constructor or `OnAppearing` throws silently**, or more commonly, the **`BindingContext` / `ItemsSource` is null**, the page appears blank.

Most likely cause: **duplicate route segments or wrong nesting** â€” the visual tree selects a tab/item but the content doesn't match. Verify your XAML hierarchy:

```xml
<Shell>
  <TabBar>
    <ShellContent Route="products" ...>  <!-- âťŚ flat â€” "active" has no parent -->
  </TabBar>
</Shell>
```

Should be:

```xml
<Shell>
  <TabBar>
    <Tab Route="products">
      <ShellContent Route="active" Title="Active"
                    ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate local:ActivePage}" />
    </Tab>
  </TabBar>
</Shell>
```

The route `//products/active` requires `products` to be a `Tab` (or `FlyoutItem`) **containing** `active` as a child `ShellContent`.

---

**3. 3-second startup with 12 pages**

Shell instantiates every page registered via `Content=` at startup. Switch to **`ContentTemplate`** with `DataTemplate` for deferred (lazy) creation:

```xml
<!-- âťŚ Eager â€” page created immediately -->
<ShellContent Route="settings">
    <local:SettingsPage />
</ShellContent>

<!-- âś… Lazy â€” page created on first navigation -->
<ShellContent Route="settings"
              ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate local:SettingsPage}" />
```

Do this for all 12 pages. Only the initially visible page will be constructed at launch; the rest are created on demand. This alone typically cuts startup time dramatically.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking about three common .NET MAUI Shell navigation issues. These are well-known patterns I can answer directly from knowledge without needing to search any codebase.
